message:
🐛 Fix warmup hang when exec credential plugin blocks indefinitely (#3058)

WarmupHealthCache probes all clusters in parallel goroutines then calls
wg.Wait(). If one cluster's GetClient() blocks forever (e.g. an OCI or
other exec-based credential helper that doesn't honour context
cancellation), wg.Wait() never returns and the server stays in
{"status":"starting"} forever.

Fix: run wg.Wait() in a goroutine and select on ctx.Done() so the 8s
overall warmup timeout always unblocks startup, even if a probe goroutine
is stuck. The hung goroutine is abandoned (it will eventually be killed
when the exec plugin times out or the process exits).

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
